steps.title: Parse the dimensions and relationships
steps.content:
A right rectangular prism's volume depends on three dimensions. From the problem:
  • Height = 9 inches
  • Length of base = $x$ inches
  • Width of base = unknown
The key relationship: length is 7 inches more than width. This translates to $x = \text{width} + 7$. Solving for width: $\text{width} = x - 7$.

steps.title: Apply the volume formula
steps.content:
Substitute all three dimensions into the volume formula:
$$V = x \times (x - 7) \times 9 = 9x(x - 7)$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — 'V(x) = x(x + 9)(x + 7)': This uses three factors but incorrectly treats 9 as a dimension alongside the base dimensions and adds 7 instead of subtracting. Width would be x + 7, not x − 7. ✗
  • Choice B — 'V(x) = x(x + 9)(x − 7)': Distributing would give a cubic expression; this mixes the height (9) as if it were a base dimension rather than multiplying separately. ✗
  • Choice C — 'V(x) = 9x(x + 7)': Width = x + 7 would mean width is 7 more than length, but the problem says length is 7 more than width, so width = x − 7, not x + 7. ✗
  • Choice D — 'V(x) = 9x(x − 7)': Height = 9, length = x, width = x − 7. Volume = 9 · x · (x − 7) = 9x(x − 7). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
